name: Drona
description: Drona, also known as Dronacharya, was a prominent figure in the epic Mahabharata. He was a revered Brahmin guru, a master of weaponry, and the royal preceptor to the Kuru princes, both the Pandavas and the Kauravas. Born from a pot after his father, Rishi Bharadwaja, was aroused by the sight of an apsara named Ghritachi, Drona possessed immense knowledge and skill in warfare. He trained Arjuna, who became his most devoted and skilled student, promising to make him the greatest archer in the world. Drona's loyalty to Hastinapura, however, often placed him in morally ambiguous situations during the Kurukshetra War. He fought on the side of the Kauravas, bound by his obligations, despite his affection for the Pandavas. His strategic brilliance and mastery of celestial weapons made him a formidable warrior, and his death, achieved through deceit orchestrated by Yudhishthira, marked a turning point in the war. Drona's story highlights themes of duty, loyalty, the complexities of dharma, and the consequences of war.
subtitle: The Master Archer and Royal Preceptor
achievements: Trained Arjuna to become the greatest archer
Became the royal preceptor of the Kuru princes
Commanded the Kaurava army in the Kurukshetra War

keyMyths.title: Birth of Drona
keyMyths.description: Drona was born from a pot after his father, Rishi Bharadwaja, was aroused by the sight of the apsara Ghritachi. This unusual birth gave him his name, 'Drona,' meaning 'born from a pot.'

keyMyths.title: Drona's Vow of Revenge
keyMyths.description: Drona was humiliated by King Drupada, who was his childhood friend, due to a change in Drupada's social status. Drona vowed revenge and later orchestrated events to claim half of Drupada's kingdom through his students.

keyMyths.title: Drona's Death
keyMyths.description: Drona was invincible as long as he held his weapons. Krishna orchestrated the death of an elephant named Ashwatthama and spread the rumor that Drona's son, Ashwatthama, was dead, leading Drona to lay down his arms in grief, making him vulnerable.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Drona and other characters in Hindu mythology.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Drona and other characters in Hindu mythology.

sources.author: C. Rajagopalachari
